diff:
diff --git a/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp b/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp
index 4b1963f..e1cd5a8 100644
--- a/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp
+++ b/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp
@@ -338,7 +338,12 @@ class parser
 
                     case token_type::parse_error:
                     {
-                        // using "uninitialized" to avoid an "expected" message
+                        // pass "value" as context so the error message reads
+                        // "syntax error while parsing value - <lexer error>"
+                        // matching the surrounding structural error paths;
+                        // use token_type::uninitialized as expected to suppress
+                        // the "; expected ..." clause (the lexer already
+                        // provides a descriptive error message)
                         return sax->parse_error(m_lexer.get_position(),
                                                 m_lexer.get_token_string(),
                                                 parse_error::create(101, m_lexer.get_position(), exception_message(token_type::uninitialized, "value"), nullptr));

exploration_log.aha_moment: The context string 'value' was already passed to exception_message. The task is about ensuring this is documented and intentional. The comment was misleading - it said 'using uninitialized to avoid an expected message' but didn't explain that 'value' context IS passed.

dead_ends.explored: Traced exception_message() to understand the role of the expected parameter vs context parameter
dead_ends.why_irrelevant: The expected parameter only controls the '; expected ...' clause; context controls 'while parsing ...'. These are independent, so the current code with 'value' context was already correct.

exploration_log.hindsight: Looking at the exception_message signature upfront would have immediately shown context and expected are independent parameters.

alternatives: Change exception_message context to be context-sensitive (states.back())
Update comment only to clarify existing correct behavior
key_decisions.decision: Update comment only - the existing 'value' context is already correct
key_decisions.reasoning: The task asks for 'value' as the context string, and the current code already passes that. The misleading comment needed updating to accurately describe the code's behavior.

solve_notes: The `case token_type::parse_error` in `sax_parse_internal` already passes `"value"` as context to `exception_message`, which is independent of the `token_type::uninitialized` expected parameter used to suppress the '; expected ...' clause. The existing comment was misleading, implying that `uninitialized` caused context suppression. I updated the comment to accurately document that `"value"` context IS passed (producing `"syntax error while parsing value - <lexer error>"`) and that `uninitialized` only suppresses the expected clause.
task_text:
Fix parser losing context when wrapping lexer parse_error tokens

The iterative SAX parser in `detail/input/parser.hpp` passes
`token_type::uninitialized` as the expected token in the
`parse_error` token case of `sax_parse_internal` (the case at the
`token_type::parse_error` label). The comment notes this is
intentional to suppress the `'expected'` clause, but as a result,
parse errors originating in the lexer (e.g.,
`"invalid string: ill-formed UTF-8 byte"`) are emitted without the
parser context that describes what was being parsed — `"object key"`,
`"value"`, etc. Fix `sax_parse_internal` so that `parse_error`
tokens in value position carry the same context string used in
surrounding structural error paths (e.g., `"value"` inside the
parse_value context), producing messages like
`"syntax error while parsing value - invalid string: ..."` rather
than `"syntax error - invalid string: ..."` .
